summary: Iteration-2 re-review of requirements.md (R1-R50) for completeness and atomicity against the ask (at-a-glance dashboard: active pipelines + phase/status, recent activity, worker fleet health), the discovery notes, and open_questions.md. Completeness: every user need implied by the ask is covered. The three panels are each fully specified -- active pipelines (R8-R18: title/project, phase + progress, plain-language status, needs-person and stuck/blocked distinction, last-active, drill-down, empty state, overflow ordering with 'see the rest'), recent activity (R19-R24: plain-language event + pipeline/project/when, newest-first, drill-down, empty state, digestible window), worker fleet (R25-R30: online/offline counts, per-worker name/state/last-heard, unhealthy flag, role-coverage warning R28, prominent-unhealthy R30, no-workers empty state). Commonly-missed edges are all present: zero-count display (R7), empty states for all three panels (R17/R23/R29), partial-load graceful degradation (R33), color-independent status (R12/R35), relative time (R32), unauthenticated redirect-and-return (R2), multi-project aggregation/labeling (R3), and no-project empty state (R4). The new human-in-the-loop modal (R36-R50), confirmed new this gate by open_questions.md, carries its negative/edge paths intact: all-defaults validation (R45), cancel-leaves-untouched (R46), busy-loop preservation (R47), keyboard/AT operability (R48), and hide-when-unanswerable (R50). Atomicity: each requirement reads as testable, observable behavior. A few bundle tightly-coupled display fields into one behavior (R6 headline counts; R10 phase+progress; R20 activity fields; R26 worker fields; R48 modal focus behaviors), but each is a single behavior verifiable by one focused test -- acceptable behavior-level atomicity for business requirements, not a defect. Considered potential gaps (initial loading/skeleton state; a generic modal-submit failure beyond the service's :busy result) and found each is covered in principle by R33's graceful-degradation rule or is implementation detail rather than a missed user need; the realistic service failure modes (busy, not-answerable) are handled by R47/R50. Genuinely under-specified quantities (which statuses = active/needs-attention, the recent-activity window/cap, staleness threshold, live-update streaming split) are correctly deferred to open_questions.md with reasoned defaults rather than left as silent gaps. No blocker, major, or minor completeness or atomicity defects found.
